channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Black Mirror season 7: everything we know so far about the hit Netflix show’s return
item.description: Black Mirror season 7 is coming in 2025 – here's everything we know about the hit Netflix show's return so far.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why tech in Congress lags behind the modern world
item.description: On a typical day, you can’t turn on the news without hearing someone say that Congress is broken. The implication is that this dereliction explains why the institution is inert and unresponsive to the American people.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ indicts North Korean conspirators for remote IT work scheme
item.description: The US Department of Justice this week announced that it had indicted two North Korean nationals and three other men, accusing them of participating in a conspiracy designed to trick US companies into funding the North Korean regime.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI’s new Operator agent can use the web for you
item.description: OpenAI is releasing a preview version of its first AI agent, Operator, which is specifically designed to use a web browser and can, for example, book a table at a restaurant for the user on its own. (An AI agent is a system that can be given a task and then work on it independently.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Smile! You can now control your Chromebook with your face
item.description: Google has started rolling out a number of new features in Chrome OS, according to The Verge, including a new feature that allows a user to control their Chromebook using head movements (to move the mouse pointer) and facial expressions (to click or activate dictation).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: More questions than answers around Trump’s Stargate AI plans
item.description: In its second day of operation, the Trump administration announced a $500 billion private sector initiative called Stargate intending to build AI infrastructure across the United States and create over 100,000 U.S. jobs. But questions about the project abound. Pres.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data center growth puts unprecedented pressure on power grids
item.description: According to a report released this week by Bloom Energy, US data centers will need 55 gigawatts of new power capacity within the next five years. The report, based on a survey of 100 data center leaders, also shows that 30% of all sites will be using onsite power by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New bill would allow California communities to decide where battery plants are located
item.description: AB 303 will remove battery energy storage facilities from the California Energy Commission’s opt-in certification program, and return the power to local communities on whether to accept new battery plants.
item.dc:creator: Kyarra Harris, The Mercury News

item.title: There’s a hidden Thread radio inside your iPhone 16 and 15 – and Apple’s just explained the 2 key things it does
item.description: A new interview with an Apple director and Thread president has shed more light on the Thread radio that's in recent iPhones.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Ubisoft reveals Assassin's Creed Shadows PC requirements – and just as I thought, you're safe with your RTX 4000 series GPU
item.description: Nvidia's new RTX 5000 GPU lineup promises to enhance gaming performance in multiple games, but PC requirements for Ubisoft's new title suggest an upgrade isn't urgently needed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: California lab makes new discovery in search for HIV vaccine
item.description: A unique reaction in which antibodies bind to other antibodies may help scientists at Scripps Research better understand how to design a vaccine
item.dc:creator: Ashley Mackin Solomon, The Mercury News

item.title: Fake Reddit sites found pushing Lumma Stealer malware
item.description: Hackers are faking Reddit threads and WeTransfer files to try and trick users into downloading malware.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: El Hospital Sant Joan de Déu mejora la atención al paciente a través de un nuevo modelo de gestión de datos
item.description: El Hospital Sant Joan de Déu ha recurrido a la tecnología de Denodo para gestionar, explotar y analizar de manera rápida, efectiva y de forma ética los datos del centro. El propósito no es otro que dar un paso adelante en la mejora de la atención y el diagnóstico de sus pacientes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sony's next flagship headphones leak in official docs, and now I'm getting excited
item.description: An FCC filing for the Sony WH-1000XM6 shows better Bluetooth and what looks like different construction too
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: TikTok France is being sued by 7 families. Here’s why
item.description: In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: ADL, which had defended Musk, takes issue with his Nazi puns
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League (ADL), a nonprofit committed to fighting antisemitism, criticized tech billionaire Elon Musk for his social media post Thursday that used Nazi puns to taunt those who accused him of making a fascist salute at an event following President Trump’s inauguration on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Ivanka Trump calls out 'fake crypto coin' named after her
item.description: Ivanka Trump, President Trump’s eldest daughter, warned Thursday that a cryptocurrency token bearing her name is fake.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taiwan plans to cull up to 120,000 green iguanas due to their negative impact on agriculture
item.description: Taiwan plans to cull up to 120,000 green iguanas, with supporters urging humane methods to bring down the animal's population, which is wreaking havoc on the island's agricultural sector.
item.dc:creator: Tai-Jing Wu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stratoshark brings Wireshark-style analysis to cloud system calls
item.description: The open-source Wireshark network protocol analyzer has been a standard tool for networking professionals for decades. In recent years, organizations have increasingly moved workloads to the cloud, where they have not had the same network visibility.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How to manage photos, videos on new laptop with limited space
item.description: With limited hard drive space, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers these tips for storing large video files and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, Fox 5
